summary: The canonical containerJobs service and managed-session capability advertisement are present, but the superseded per-session Docker sidecar remains broadly implemented and supported in schemas, session state, controller lifecycle, Temporal activities, preflight, settings, tests, and documentation. The checkout is zero commits ahead of origin/main and no recent merge referencing #3264 was found. Substantial new code work is required.

requirements.description: Remove the supported docker-sidecar capability, session-local Docker endpoint, graph/socket volumes, sidecar resources, and related profile validators; these remain in moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py and its tests.
Stop advertising sidecar readiness and DOCKER_HOST in managed-session state while advertising containerJobs; containerJobs is advertised, but sidecar capability/status models and dockerHost remain.
Remove DinD create/start/probe/restart/reap behavior and Docker-authority injection from managed_session_controller.py; the controller still implements all of these paths.
Remove agent_runtime.ensure_docker_sidecar and related Temporal catalog, handler, routing, and controller construction; the activity remains registered and implemented.
Remove session-side Docker CLI/Compose preflight; moonmind/workflows/automation/preflight.py still detects docker-sidecar/DOCKER_HOST and emits preflight://docker-sidecar.

Remove or justify sidecar-only settings, compose wiring, credentials, image preflight, schemas, tests, status fields, and lifecycle code; extensive sidecar-specific production and test references remain.
Support generic container jobs through MoonMind MCP/API with no sidecar present; the container-job API/MCP service exists, but managed sessions still support and launch sidecars.
Remove every user/operator-selectable sidecar compatibility/runtime mode; docker-sidecar and docker-sidecar-rootless vocabulary remains accepted or handled.

Add replay/payload compatibility code only when evidence-backed and time-bounded, otherwise add none; existing legacy payload migration and sidecar model support are not documented as the narrow evidence-backed guard required by the issue.

Provide conservative legacy sidecar cleanup that identifies MoonMind-owned inactive resources and never deletes images or unrelated objects; current sidecar orphan/reaper code and tests show ownership-aware cleanup elements, but the required one-time post-removal reconciliation path is not established.
Leave no supported sidecar implementation references outside tombstones or bounded migration guards; repository search finds many active production, test, and canonical-doc references.

Keep Omnigent-host and managed-session containers without Docker authority; documentation and no-Docker paths cover this goal, but selectable managed-session sidecar paths still inject DOCKER_HOST and socket volumes.
